title: campanula bertolae var. re
intro: Campanula bertolae var. re, also known as the alpine bellflower, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Campanulaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, providing several health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and protecting against infections. The alpine bellflower can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, which are typically used to treat skin conditions, promote wound healing, and support overall health.
